global_options.name: refresh
global_options.description: Force a refresh of VIPM's package list before performing the operation

global_options.name: labview_version
global_options.description: Performs the operation on or using the specified LabVIEW version. Accepts only YYYY format (4-digit year, e.g., 2020, 2024, 2025). Use --labview-bitness for 32/64-bit selection

global_options.name: labview_bitness
global_options.description: Used in conjunction with --labview-version when there are multiple bitnesses of the same LabVIEW version installed

global_options.name: color
global_options.description: Control when to use colored output

global_options.name: global_timeout
global_options.description: Maximum time (in seconds) to wait for the operation to complete. Use -1 to wait indefinitely. The default varies by command and is longer in CI environments than locally

global_options.name: show_progress
global_options.description: Show real-time progress from VIPM Desktop during operations

global_options.name: verbose
global_options.description: Show verbose diagnostic output (API calls, timing, file paths)

commands.name: about
commands.description: Prints information about the VIPM installation

commands.name: activate
commands.description: Activates VIPM Pro

options.name: serial_number
options.description: Serial Number (will prompt if not provided in interactive mode)

options.name: name
options.description: Your full name (will prompt if not provided in interactive mode)

options.name: email
options.description: Your email address (will prompt if not provided in interactive mode)

commands.name: install
commands.description: Installs packages from vipm.toml, .vipc, .dragon files, or by name
commands.after_help:
VIPM.TOML SUPPORT:
  When run without arguments, auto-detects project manifests in this order:
    1. vipm.toml (highest priority)
    2. .dragon file
    3. .vipc file

  For vipm.toml projects, the labview_version from [project] is used automatically.
  Dev-dependencies ([dev-dependencies]) can be included with --dev.
  Path and git dependencies are also skipped with a warning.

EXAMPLES:
  vipm install                      # Auto-detect vipm.toml, .dragon, or .vipc
  vipm install ./vipm.toml          # Install from specific vipm.toml
  vipm install --dev ./vipm.toml    # Install regular + dev dependencies from vipm.toml
  vipm install project.vipc         # Install from .vipc file
  vipm install oglib_array          # Install a package by name
  vipm install oglib_array@6.0.1.20 # Install specific version

arguments.name: packages
arguments.description: Packages to install by name (e.g., "oglib_array"), name with version ("oglib_array@6.0.1.20"), or path to a .vip, .ogp, .vipc, .dragon, or vipm.toml file. If no packages are specified and a vipm.toml, .dragon, or .vipc file exists, it will be used

options.name: dev
options.description: Install dev-dependencies from vipm.toml in addition to regular dependencies

options.name: no_dev
options.description: Skip dev-dependencies (explicit toggle for clarity)

options.name: upgrade
options.description: Upgrade packages to the latest version if already installed

options.name: yes
options.description: Skip confirmation prompt when installing from files

options.name: best_effort
options.description: Continue when dev-dependency resolution fails (warns instead of errors). Lets a production install proceed even if a dev-dependency version, feed, or spec is temporarily unresolvable

options.name: vipm
options.description: Include only VIPM packages

options.name: nipm
options.description: Include only NIPM packages

options.name: no_vipm
options.description: Exclude VIPM packages from results

options.name: no_nipm
options.description: Exclude NIPM packages from results

commands.name: build
commands.description: Builds project artifacts from various sources

arguments.name: build_spec
arguments.description: Build name from vipm.toml or path to a .lvproj/.vipb file. If omitted, builds all default targets from vipm.toml

options.name: all
options.description: Build all targets defined in vipm.toml (including those with default=false)

options.name: lvproj_build_spec
options.description: Name of the build specification within a LabVIEW project file (only for .lvproj files)

options.name: lvproj_target
options.description: Scope build-spec lookup to a specific target within the .lvproj file

options.name: version_number
options.description: Set the version number (1-4 components: major.minor.patch[.build]); missing build preserves the existing build number when present

options.name: build_number
options.description: Set the build number (4th component of version). Applies to .lvproj and .vipb; without --version-number, existing major.minor.patch values are reused (defaulting to 0.1.0 when none are present)

options.name: debug
options.description: Build in debug mode (preserves block diagrams and front panels)

options.name: no_deps
options.description: Skip building dependencies (assume they already exist)

options.name: rebuild_deps
options.description: Force rebuild all dependencies (clean and rebuild even if outputs exist)

commands.name: clean
commands.description: Remove build output directories defined in vipm.toml

arguments.name: build
arguments.description: Build name to clean (defaults to all builds when omitted)

options.name: all
options.description: Clean all builds defined in vipm.toml

options.name: dry_run
options.description: Show what would be removed without deleting files

options.name: yes
options.description: Skip confirmation prompt

commands.name: init
commands.description: Initialize a new vipm.toml file

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: Directory to initialize (defaults to current directory)

options.name: name
options.description: Set the project name

options.name: force
options.description: Overwrite existing vipm.toml

commands.name: add
commands.description: Add dependencies to vipm.toml

arguments.name: packages
arguments.description: Packages to add by name (e.g., "oglib_array") or name with version ("oglib_array@6.0.1.20")

options.name: install
options.description: Download, install, and update vipm.lock after editing vipm.toml

options.name: dev
options.description: Add as dev dependency instead of regular dependency

options.name: nipm
options.description: Add NIPM package (writes to [nipm.dependencies] or [nipm.dev-dependencies])

options.name: feed
options.description: NIPM feed alias (optional; vipm will attempt auto-resolution if omitted)

options.name: feed_url
options.description: NIPM feed URL to add when the alias does not yet exist in vipm.toml

commands.name: remove
commands.description: Remove dependencies from vipm.toml

options.name: dev
options.description: Remove from dev dependencies instead of regular dependencies

options.name: nipm
options.description: Remove NIPM packages (targets [nipm.dependencies] or [nipm.dev-dependencies])

commands.name: lock
commands.description: Generate or check vipm.lock from vipm.toml

options.name: check
options.description: Check if vipm.lock is in sync without writing changes

options.name: best_effort
options.description: Continue with incomplete lock file when specs cannot be fetched (warns instead of errors)

commands.name: list
commands.description: Lists packages from a configuration file or shows installed packages

arguments.name: file
arguments.description: Lists packages declared in the specified .vipc or .dragon file

options.name: installed
options.description: Lists all packages installed in the current LabVIEW version

options.name: dev
options.description: Show only dev dependencies (vipm.toml)

options.name: no_dev
options.description: Hide dev dependencies (vipm.toml)

options.name: vipm
options.description: Include only VIPM packages

options.name: nipm
options.description: Include only NIPM packages

options.name: no_vipm
options.description: Exclude VIPM packages from results

options.name: no_nipm
options.description: Exclude NIPM packages from results

commands.name: info
commands.description: Show metadata and installed files for a package

options.name: installed_files
options.description: List the files installed by the package

options.name: nipm
options.description: Query an NIPM package instead of a VIPM package

commands.name: search
commands.description: Searches for available packages by name or description

options.name: limit
options.description: Limit the number of packages shown (default: 10)

commands.name: uninstall
commands.description: Uninstalls one or more packages

arguments.name: packages
arguments.description: Package names or file paths (.vipc, .dragon) to uninstall

options.name: allow_version_mismatch
options.description: Allow uninstalling when installed version differs from file specification

options.name: yes
options.description: Skip confirmation prompt when uninstalling from files

commands.name: sbom
commands.description: Generate a Software Bill of Materials

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Path to a vipm.toml, .dragon, .vipc, or .lvproj file (default: vipm.toml)

options.name: format
options.description: SBOM output format

options.name: schema_version
options.description: CycloneDX schema version

options.name: document_version
options.description: SBOM revision number (default: 1)

options.name: document_serial_number
options.description: Unique identifier for this BOM's lineage (urn:uuid:... format)

options.name: output
options.description: Output file path

options.name: product_version
options.description: Version of the software described by the SBOM

options.name: product_name
options.description: Name of the software described by the SBOM (overrides the filename-derived name)

options.name: product_type
options.description: Component type of the software described by the SBOM (default: application)

options.name: lvproj_build_spec
options.description: Name of a build specification within the .lvproj file. Extracts product name, version, and type from the build spec as defaults for metadata.component (overridden by explicit --product-* flags). The containing target is inferred automatically when unambiguous; pass --lvproj-target to disambiguate if the same name exists under multiple targets

options.name: lvproj_target
options.description: Scope the dependency scan to a specific .lvproj target. May be used alone (scans only that target) or with --lvproj-build-spec (validates the spec is under that target). Omit to scan the whole project

options.name: no_dev
options.description: Exclude dev-dependencies from SBOM output

options.name: vipm
options.description: Include only VIPM packages

options.name: nipm
options.description: Include only NIPM packages

options.name: no_vipm
options.description: Exclude VIPM packages from results

options.name: no_nipm
options.description: Exclude NIPM packages from results

options.name: allow_package_drift
options.description: Allow SBOM generation to proceed when installed packages disagree with `vipm.toml` / `vipm.lock`. A stderr warning lists the drifted packages and the SBOM reflects installed versions. Without this flag, drift aborts with exit code 17 (`PACKAGE_DRIFT`)

options.name: allow_missing_files
options.description: Allow SBOM generation to proceed when files referenced by the project cannot be found on disk. A stderr warning lists the missing references and the SBOM reflects what could be scanned. Without this flag, missing references abort with exit code 18 (`MISSING_REFERENCED_FILES`)

commands.name: sync
commands.description: Synchronize vipm.toml with dependencies discovered by scanning a project

arguments.name: target
arguments.description: Manifest to update (default: vipm.toml, searched up through parent folders)

options.name: from
options.description: Project file to scan for dependencies

options.name: dry_run
options.description: Show what would change without writing to disk

options.name: vipm
options.description: Include only VIPM packages

options.name: nipm
options.description: Include only NIPM packages

options.name: no_vipm
options.description: Exclude VIPM packages from results

options.name: no_nipm
options.description: Exclude NIPM packages from results

options.name: allow_missing_files
options.description: Allow sync to proceed when files referenced by the project cannot be found on disk. A stderr warning lists the missing references and sync operates on what could be resolved. Without this flag, missing references abort with exit code 18 (`MISSING_REFERENCED_FILES`)

commands.name: refresh
commands.description: Refresh all package sources (VIPM Desktop, CLI cache, NIPM feeds)

options.name: no_cache
options.description: Skip CLI cache update (repo indexes, specs, database)

options.name: no_nipm
options.description: Skip NIPM feed update

options.name: force
options.description: Force re-download even if cached

commands.name: version
commands.description: Prints the version of the VIPM CLI and VIPM Desktop

commands.name: login
commands.description: Log in to vipm.io

options.name: email
options.description: Email for vipm.io

options.name: password
options.description: Password for vipm.io (will prompt if not provided in interactive mode)

options.name: token
options.description: Provide an auth token directly instead of email/password

options.name: no_store
options.description: Do not persist the token locally (always implied in CI)

options.name: allow_file_store
options.description: Allow insecure file-based token storage if keyring is unavailable (not recommended)

commands.name: whoami
commands.description: Show the current vipm.io account

options.name: token
options.description: Provide an auth token directly instead of using stored credentials

options.name: no_store
options.description: Do not persist or consult local token storage (always implied in CI)

options.name: allow_file_store
options.description: Allow insecure file-based token storage if keyring is unavailable (not recommended)

commands.name: logout
commands.description: Log out of vipm.io locally (clears stored token)

options.name: no_store
options.description: Do not attempt to clear local storage (CI/no-store)

options.name: allow_file_store
options.description: Allow insecure file-based token storage if keyring is unavailable (not recommended)
